widgets_values: A woman is giving a speech. She is confident, poised, and joyful. Use a static shot.

increase audio_scale for stronger effect

audio_cfg is only used when sampler is using cfg as well

num_frames is the maximum frame count to generate, if the value is higher than your audio length would be, the audio length is used

Clip vision is not strictly necessary

Any I2V model should work, MAGREF can be interesting to play with as well.

nodes.title: Wav2vec2 safetensors
widgets_values:
Alternative to the download node is to use single .safetensors file from:

[https://huggingface.co/Kijai/wav2vec2_safetensors/tree/main](https://huggingface.co/Kijai/wav2vec2_safetensors/tree/main)

The model goes to `ComfyUI/models/wav2vec2`

nodes.title: Wav2vec2 safetensors
widgets_values:
Vocal separator model

[https://huggingface.co/Kijai/MelBandRoFormer_comfy/tree/main](https://huggingface.co/Kijai/MelBandRoFormer_comfy/tree/main)

The model goes to `ComfyUI/models/diffusion_models`

This node makes the sampling into a loop to process all the needed frames. Each window is the same size, if the frame count doesn't divide evenly the audio will be padded with silence and extra frames generated.

Between each window the frames have to be re-encoded, thus no latents are saved and end result is already decoded.

You can also set an output_path to save each window's result as images during the process.

Hello everyone. So in today's video, I'm sharing a little tricks on how to use Hartmula transcript and LTX2 image to video features and make them work for AI video creation, and we can get a better for multiple angles camera motion, like in this example. Plus, you can also use your own audio input. That means you're not restricted only to the text prompts you put into LTX video. Remember how we talked about Hartmula in the last video? The custom nodes I made offer a really easy, clean way to create music with this AI model. And by the way, those custom nodes have been actively developed and keep improving, thanks to the GitHub community. Seriously, a lot of people are helping out. Now the current updated versions can run on a 12 gigabyte VRAM GPU, thanks to memory optimizations, and they also include some extra features. Extra features suggested by both the GitHub community and my Patreon community. So right now, I'm using the latest version of these custom nodes to play around with this audio. We've got all the lyrics and stuff, so we're capturing the full sound of the song. That vocal, that's the key thing we're bringing into LTX. So let's jump into the LTX workflow.
